title: Akbar, Genghis Khan and ironically Stalin: 8 people richer than Elon Musk
content: When we marvel at Elon Musk's astonishing trillion-dollar net worth, largely bolstered by SpaceX's remarkable achievements, it’s essential to draw parallels with history's most influential monarchs. Leaders like Mansa Musa, Genghis Khan, and Akbar the Great presided over vast empires whose economic legacies, when adjusted for inflation, dwarf Musk’s wealth.

title: Middle East peace 24 hours away? Pakistani PM suggests US-Iran may finalise deal soon
content: Pakistan's PM Shehbaz Sharif hinted that a peace deal between the US and Iran could be finalized within 24 hours, marking a significant diplomatic breakthrough. Both nations have indicated progress, with an agreed-upon text reportedly reached. US officials, however, urge caution against speculation, emphasizing that economic benefits for Iran are contingent on fulfilling commitments.

title: Canada's sixth-largest state Manitoba retires student pathway to permanent residency
content: Manitoba has closed its Career Employment Pathway for international graduates, effective immediately. Candidates with active EOIs and six months of provincial work experience can now be considered under the Skilled Worker in Manitoba Pathway. This change aims to align graduates' skills with labor market needs, prioritizing those who studied and are working in the province.

title: Bullets rain at Chandigarh store: Cashier shot dead, 13 rounds fired; CCTV footage emerges
content: Unidentified assailants opened fire at a medical store in Chandigarh's Sector 11, critically injuring cashier Janki Das, who later died. Two masked men entered the store and fired multiple rounds before fleeing with a third accomplice waiting outside on a motorcycle. Police are investigating, collecting CCTV footage, and have set up roadblocks.

title: 'Baseless': Iran lashes out after US blames Tehran of attacking ships with Indians onboard
content: Iran's embassy in India has dismissed US President Trump's accusation of a drone attack on Indian vessels in the Strait of Hormuz as "baseless." The embassy stated that Washington is attempting to divert attention from recent US strikes on three Indian-crewed ships, which resulted in the deaths of three Indian sailors.

title: US ban on Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 has an Amazon 'link'
content: The US government has ordered AI firm Anthropic to halt access to two of its models. This decision stems from national security concerns. Anthropic disagrees, citing a limited jailbreak technique. Amazon researchers reportedly demonstrated this technique. The company states the identified vulnerabilities were minor and already known. The shutdown affects users worldwide.

title: Grand Congress reunification? Raut's big appeal to NCP (SP), other 'breakaway' leaders
content: Shiv Sena (UBT) leader Sanjay Raut urged former Congress allies to reunite, emphasizing the need to strengthen the party against the BJP. He dismissed claims of the Congress being a "sinking ship" and appealed to Sharad Pawar to lead the reunification efforts. Raut also criticized the BJP's historical contributions and accused Amit Shah of attempting to break opposition parties.

title: Harsh Dubey, Gurnoor Brar handed maiden ODI caps as India opt to field
content: India handed ODI caps to Harsh Dubey and Gurnoor Brar for their clash against Afghanistan. Despite initial weather concerns, the match will proceed as a 25-over per side contest. Captain Shubman Gill won the toss and opted to bowl first, aiming to test new combinations ahead of the World Cup. Afghanistan, though preferring to bowl, expressed confidence in their batting.

title: 'Male corpse private parts' joke row: MBBS student barred from entering college
content: KEM Hospital suspended MBBS student Sejal Pawar for 15 days following a controversial stand-up comedy video. An FIR has been filed against her and others for allegedly circulating objectionable content. The college cited her safety and well-being amid public backlash, recommending counseling and a detailed probe by a five-member committee.

title: Kevin O'Leary says the best career advice he got were 3 things Steve Jobs told him
content: Apple founder Steve Jobs' core career advice, 'get three things done daily,' was revealed by Kevin O'Leary. Jobs emphasized separating 'signal' from 'noise,' a principle O'Leary adopted, leading to his success.  Fellow investor Robert Herjavec agreed, noting purpose drives the wealthy. This focus aligns with modern productivity research, highlighting the importance of discipline and identifying key priorities for achievement.

title: 'Vijay on shopping spree of AIADMK MLAs': Stalin calls TVK govt BJP's 'xerox copy'
content: DMK president M.K. Stalin accused Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay's government of acting as a "xerox copy" of the BJP, alleging attempts to weaken the opposition by inducting AIADMK MLAs. Stalin criticized the government's approach to women's safety, claiming TVK functionaries were involved in recent crimes and that the party removed victims instead of taking action.

title: 'As soon as Rohit retires...': Sehwag reveals who will inherit India's opening slot
content: Virender Sehwag backs Yashasvi Jaiswal to become a regular in India's ODI side post-Rohit Sharma's retirement. Despite strong performances, including a century against South Africa, Jaiswal faces tough competition. Sehwag and Manoj Tiwary believe Jaiswal is poised to lead the future opening lineup once senior players depart after next year's World Cup.

title: Mouni Roy reacts to trolls after divorce: 'I'm not gay'
content: Mouni Roy has addressed persistent online rumors about her sexual orientation following her separation from Suraj Nambiar. The actress highlighted the crucial support from her female friends during this period, emphasizing the importance of emotional and financial independence for women. Roy described her life as a 'roller-coaster ride' filled with both highs and lows.

title: Brain fog is on the rise: Why more people are struggling to focus, remember, and think clearly
content: Many are experiencing increased mental fatigue, distraction, and forgetfulness, commonly known as brain fog. Experts attribute this rise to chronic stress, insufficient sleep, excessive screen time, and sedentary lifestyles. Underlying medical conditions can also contribute. Simple daily habits like adequate sleep, exercise, and stress management can help clear the fog and protect cognitive health.

title: Two Agniveervayus among 5 killed in IAF AN-32 crash in Assam
content: Five Indian Air Force personnel tragically lost their lives in an An-32 aircraft accident near Jorhat, Assam, during a routine sortie. The aircraft crashed outside the airstrip, prompting an ongoing investigation into the cause. The IAF has requested that speculation be avoided until preliminary results are available.

title: Venezuela oil rush: US investors chase Trump's $100 billion call
content: US investment groups are actively pursuing opportunities in Venezuela's oil sector following the lifting of sanctions and a new hydrocarbons law. Lionheart Capital is leading the charge with a proposed merger to create the first Nasdaq-listed company offering direct access to Venezuelan oil assets, aiming for a $1 billion valuation.

title: How rich is a trillionaire? Musk can sponsor IPL for 17k years, spend Rs 1cr daily for 23,500 yrs
content: Elon Musk's ascent to trillionaire status, driven by Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI, redefines immense wealth. A trillion dollars, approximately Rs 86 lakh crore, dwarfs national economies and government budgets. This fortune could fund multiple Apollo missions, build numerous advanced telescopes, or purchase over 75 aircraft carriers, illustrating wealth on a scale previously unimaginable.

title: Zlatan Ibrahimovic kicks IShowSpeed off stream over Ronaldo World Cup take
content: The FIFA World Cup 2026 has already delivered plenty of memorable moments. One such incident came during Fox Sports’ coverage of the United States versus Paraguay match when former Sweden striker Zlatan Ibrahimovic shared the studio with French legend Thierry Henry and popular streamer Speed. What started as a routine discussion quickly turned into a viral social media moment that left viewers entertained.

title: Madhya Pradesh's 'Scheduled Caste Boys/Girls Housing Assistance' scheme: Eligibility, benefits and FAQs
content: Madhya Pradesh's 'Scheduled Caste Boys/Girls Housing Assistance Scheme' offers monthly rental stipends up to Rs. 2,000 for SC students studying outside their hometown. Eligibility requires MP domicile, SC status, and adherence to income limits. Applications are submitted online via the MPTAAS portal, with funds directly transferred via DBT. The scheme exclusively benefits MP residents in rented accommodation.

title: 'New levels of frustration': BJP counters Sanjay Raut's 'aghori' remark on PM Modi
content: BJP strongly countered Shiv Sena (UBT) leader Sanjay Raut's remarks comparing PM Modi to Aurangzeb, accusing the opposition of frustration and insulting Gujarat. Raut's sharp attack questioned Modi's character, calling him "cruel" and from the "land of Aurangzeb," sparking immediate BJP condemnation for crossing political discourse limits.

title: Standard helmets & seat belts could have saved thousands of lives on roads in 2024
content: As per a UN Motorcycle Helmet Study, motorcyclists are 26 times more likely to die in road crashes than drivers of cars and wearing an appropriate helmet “improves their chances of survival by 42% and helps avoid 69% of injuries to riders.” Similarly, as per World Health Organisation, seat-belts are approximately 50% effective in preventing fatalities in crashes in which motorists would otherwise die.

title: Dharamsala rain: What is the cut-off time for IND vs AFG 1st ODI match?
content: Persistent rain continues to disrupt the first ODI between India and Afghanistan in Dharamshala, with overs already being lost and the cut-off time for a 20-over contest set at 6:32 pm. The toss remains delayed as players wait for conditions to improve, with weather forecasts offering hope of clearer skies later in the evening.

title: 'Routine sortie': IAF on what led to An-32 aircraft crash in Assam that killed 5
content: Tragedy struck Jorhat, Assam, as an Indian Air Force An-32 aircraft crashed during a routine sortie, claiming the lives of five personnel. The IAF confirmed the identities of the deceased, including Squadron Leader Prashant Singh and Flight Lieutenant Shubham Kumar. Investigations are underway, and the nation mourns the supreme sacrifice made by these brave air warriors.

title: SpaceX's $75 billion IPO could get an $11 billion boost — here's how
content: SpaceX's IPO raised $75 billion, with a greenshoe option potentially adding another $11.2 billion. This mechanism allows underwriters to buy additional shares from the company if the stock price rises above the IPO price, boosting the total funds raised. The next few weeks will reveal if SpaceX's landmark fundraising event grows larger.

title: Water tankers on way to parched localities spill thousands of litres in Delhi
content: Delhi's water tankers are losing significant amounts of water before reaching thirsty households.  Leakages at filling points and during transit, due to old vehicles and poor infrastructure, mean less water for residents already struggling with supply.  Authorities promise to address this wastage, acknowledging water's immense value.

title: Hyderabad woman jumps to death from 6th floor with 6-month-old baby in arms; infant survives
content: A 37-year-old woman tragically died after jumping from the sixth floor of a Hyderabad residential building while holding her infant daughter. The six-month-old survived with minor injuries. Police stated the woman had been experiencing depression in recent months.

title: What happens when online gaming addiction blurs the line between the virtual world and reality?
content: Digital India's youth find entertainment and social connection in online gaming, but excessive screen time is leading to family conflicts, disrupted lives, and concerning incidents.  Experts highlight the complex interplay of emotional needs, social change, and the design of modern games, urging for balance and early intervention.

title: How the actor who inspired Amitabh Bachchan and Rishi Kapoor died in a chawl
content: From the dusty lanes of a mill worker to the glittering lights of Bollywood, Bhagwan Dada captivated audiences with his mesmerizing dance in the film 'Albela.' His journey inspired icons like Amitabh Bachchan, a testament to his influence in Hindi cinema. However, the peaks of fame were fleeting; his fortune faded due to missteps and personal turmoil.

title: Looking to transfer unregistered inherited property? Here's how you can save lakhs
content: Uttar Pradesh RERA has capped flat transfer fees for legal heirs inheriting unregistered property at Rs. 1,000, a significant relief from exorbitant developer charges. For non-family successors, the fee is capped at Rs. 25,000. This move aims to bring transparency and affordability to property inheritance transfers, requiring a death certificate, succession certificate, and NOC from other heirs.

title: 'Denied favourable work assignments': How managers 'drove' Pune techie to suicide
content: Pimpri-Chinchwad Police have registered a case against three individuals, including two TCS employees, following the alleged suicide of IT professional Amit Brahme. His son's complaint details prolonged mental harassment, humiliation, and defamation, citing a suicide note that names a friend and two colleagues for workplace pressure and false accusations. Investigations are ongoing.

title: 'Abhishek is like my son': Kalyan Banerjee softens tone after calling TMC leader 'arrogant'
content: TMC leader Kalyan Banerjee softened his stance on Abhishek Banerjee, calling him 'like his son' after recent criticism. This comes amid a significant internal party crisis with several MPs seeking separate recognition. Banerjee also dismissed the threat posed by rebel MPs, accusing them of aligning with the BJP and facing harassment.

title: Delhiites to pay more for power from next month
content: Delhi power bills are set to increase for lakhs of consumers as the DERC allows discoms to recover higher fuel and power purchase costs. Consumers in BYPL areas will see a 5.7% rise, while BRPL consumers face a 3.4% increase, effective from July. This hike follows a sharp rise in procurement costs due to increased demand and expensive market purchases.

title: 'Lose to anyone, but not Pakistan': Jemimah ahead of IND vs PAK opener
content: Ahead of India’s Women’s T20 World Cup opener against Pakistan, Jemimah Rodrigues said the rivalry brings immense pressure, recalling how even her building watchman stresses the importance of beating Pakistan. Despite the expectations, she said the team remains focused on winning its maiden T20 World Cup title, with extensive preparation and mental conditioning boosting confidence.

title: 'Mega bubble or fairy tale?': Uday Kotak reacts to SpaceX's historic IPO
content: Veteran banker Uday Kotak questioned SpaceX's massive valuation, calling it a "huge bet on the future" that defies traditional metrics.  Elon Musk's company debuted on Wall Street, making him the world's first trillionaire.  Despite concerns about unproven technologies and cash burn, investor faith remains strong, highlighting a debate on future value versus current metrics.

title: 'Please give me one chance': Pranit More apologises over viral Rs 370 biryani remark
content: Comedian Pranit More has apologized for his 'Rs 370 biryani' remark controversy, admitting a lapse in judgment for not intervening during a show where derogatory comments were made. He expressed remorse for offending people and is cooperating with authorities investigating the viral clips for alleged obscenity and violations of public decency.

title: England script history, smash Women’s T20 World Cup record in Sri Lanka rout
content: England shattered Women's T20 World Cup records with a colossal 219/1, powered by Danni Wyatt-Hodge's unbeaten 105. A dominant batting display, including a record opening stand, set a daunting target. Sri Lanka crumbled under pressure, bowled out for 132 as Freya Kemp claimed a career-best 4 wickets, securing a resounding victory for the hosts.

title: Kidney disease: New drug shows tremendous promise; offers hope beyond diabetes
content: Finerenone, a revolutionary medication initially aimed at diabetic kidney disease, now shines a light of hope for countless individuals struggling with chronic kidney disease (CKD) independent of diabetes. Recent research indicates that it can significantly decelerate kidney deterioration, diminish failure risks, and reduce heart-related issues, potentially saving lives in a demographic often overlooked by traditional treatments.

title: FIFA World Cup: Harry Kane, Jude Bellingham gear stolen before England vs Croatia
content: England’s preparations for the FIFA World Cup 2026 have been rocked by a shocking theft involving crucial team equipment. Reports suggest that match boots belonging to some of the squad’s biggest stars, including Harry Kane and Jude Bellingham, were among several items stolen while being transported to the team’s training base in Kansas City. Official World Cup footballs and other essential training gear were also reportedly taken.

title: "Like Son": Kalyan Banerjee On Abhishek Banerjee After "Him Or Me" Ultimatum
content: Trinamool Congress MP Kalyan Banerjee's ultimatum directed at Mamata Banerjee came after her nephew Abhishek Banerjee changed his lawyer in a signature forgery case

title: Aim, Then Boom: Trump Shares Footage Of Strike On Venezuelan Cartel Leader
content: In a post on Truth Social, Trump said that the "infamous leader" of Tren de Aragua, Hector Rusthenford Guerrero Flores, also known as Nino Guerrero, was "successfully" executed.

title: Pak's Shehbaz Sharif Says US-Iran Deal Likely To Be Finalised In 24 Hours
content: Weeks of negotiations on a peace agreement after an April 8 truce have so far not yielded success, with US President Donald Trump repeatedly insisting a deal is near.

title: College Sends Medical Student On 15-Day Forced Leave After Cadaver Remark Row
content: A five-member panel has been appointed to probe the remarks that triggered nationwide outrage. It will submit a probe report within seven days.

title: Automatic Weapons, 13 Bullets: Daring Chandigarh Murder Caught On Camera
content: The attack took place at around 2:30 pm. Three attackers arrived at the shop on a motorcycle; two entered.

title: Iran's Ali Khamenei To Be Buried In Hometown Mashhad On July 9
content: The burial in Ali Khamenei's hometown, the northeastern holy city of Mashhad, initially scheduled for March but postponed due to the war

title: 15 Weeks, 5,000 Strikes: How US-Iran War Spiralled Across Middle East
content: While Iran and the Middle East are paying a heavy price for the loss of men and material, the US, too, is paying a fortune to continue the war.

title: Mag 7 To MANGOS? SpaceX May Push Wall Street To Rename Tech Stock Bundle
content: The IPO, the biggest in US history, vaulted SpaceX's value above two Mag 7 members: CEO Elon Musk's other company, Tesla, and Meta Platforms.

title: No New Aadhaar For Those Above 18 In Assam Without Extra Verification Step
content: The decision is part of the Assam government's efforts to prevent suspected illegal immigrants from securing Aadhaar documents through fraudulent means and to reinforce scrutiny over identity verification

title: US-Iran War Live: "Finalisation Likely Expected In The Next 24 Hours", Pak PM On US-Iran Deal
content: US-Israel-Iran War News Live Updates: Trump said key points of a potential agreement have been worked out with support from several countries, raising hopes of a deal soon.

title: Siachen Runs To Deadly Accidents: History Of AN-32 Which Crashed Today
content: The IAF currently operates around 100 AN-32 aircraft, which continue to form the main element of its tactical transport capability.

title: A Owaisi Meets Poll Officer, Seeks Transparency In Voter List Revision
content: Asaduddin Owaisi said his party has raised concerns over the extensive pre-enumeration exercise already conducted by election authorities.

title: Gold Worth Rs 4 Crore Flies From Dubai To Ahmedabad In Plane's Toilet Speaker
content: The seizure was made at Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel International (SVPI) Airport on Friday, on board IndiGo flight 6E-1478, which had arrived from Dubai.

title: Student Picks IIT Mandi Over Jaipur College, Court Orders 1.2 Lakh Fee Refund
content: The bench ordered the institute to refund the entire amount of Rs 94,315. In addition, the institute has been directed to pay Rs 10,000 as compensation for mental harassment and Rs 15,000 towards legal expenses.

title: Mission Sudarshan Chakra To Boost India's Missile Defence: Rajnath Singh
content: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh unveils Mission Sudarshan Chakra as India’s multi-layer missile defence system.

title: "Fake": India Denies Attack On Vessel MT Liaki Freedom Off Oman Coast
content: New Delhi, June 13 (IANS) The Ministry of External Affairs dismissed reports of attacks on an Indian-crewed tanker, MT Liaki Freedom, off the coast of Oman, which had triggered fresh concerns amid a series of such incidents in the region, clarifying

title: Two Phones And An App: How Russians Skirt Putin's Digital Iron Curtain
content: In a quiet cafe popular for its free WiFi and good coffee, a Russian interior designer logs onto a virtual private network so she can chat with friends

title: 'Safe And Sound': World Nuclear Body Chief On India's Fast Breeder Reactor
content: In an exclusive interview with NDTV's Pallava Bagla, Rafael M Grossi offered a rare and candid assessment of India's nuclear strategy

title: Politics of performance has taken centre stage under Modi govt.: Education Minister Pradhan
content: People's aspirations are very high in the age of social media, but their faith in the ability of Prime Minister Modi and his government to fulfil these aspirations is equally strong, he said

title: Rohit Pawar's indefinite fast to seek removal of conditions from farm loan waiver continues on Day 2
content: Under the waiver component, farmers whose total outstanding short-term crop loans, including principal and interest, are up to ₹2 lakh will be eligible for a complete debt relief

title: Kerala college sets up a giant portrait of football legends, made entirely of scrap
content: Artwork, spanning around 1,000 sq ft set up on Nirmala College campus in Muvattupuzha in Ernakulam district, features Lionel Messi, Cristiano Ronaldo and Neymar Junior and has been created using discarded materials, including 1.06 lakh soft drink caps

title: Maharashtra govt. considering 'single parent child' category in college admission forms: Minister
content: Citing the lack of a separate mechanism for recording children of single mothers in higher education, Chandrakant Patil said instructions have been issued to establish a proper and accurate system at the college level

title: Congress not a sinking ship, says Sanjay Raut
content: If we truly want to fight against the perverted politics of the BJP, everyone must unite; the Congress party must become stronger, says Rajya Sabha MP and Shiv Sena (UBT) leader Sanjay Raut

title: Plus One admissions in Kerala to begin on Monday
content: First allotment will be published to enable student admissions from 10 a.m. The admissions will continue till 5 p.m. on June 17. Details will be available on https://www.hscap.kerala.gov.in

title: Five IAF personnel killed in AN-32 aircraft accident in Assam's Jorhat
content: The Indian Air Force says a court of inquiry is being constituted to ascertain the cause of the mishap at the Jorhat airbase

title: 2020 Delhi riots: Sharjeel Imam, Umar Khalid move fresh bail pleas in ‘larger conspiracy’ case
content: The applications argued that there has been no significant progress in the trial despite the Supreme Court dismissing the earlier plea over six months ago

title: Government school students account for 96.7% of RGUKT admissions
content: University releases provisional list of 4,040 candidates for admission to its six-year integrated B.Tech programme across four campuses

title: Stop being facilitator for ED, Modi govt: CPI(M) slams Rahul Gandhi over remarks on Vijayan
content: Mr. Gandhi earlier said he “cannot hug” former Kerala Chief Minister Vijayan as he has an ongoing political fight with him

title: 61-day fishing ban to end in Rameswaram
content: Though the fishing ban period will end at midnight on Sunday, a decision on issuing tokens is yet to be taken due to the weather forecast of strong winds in place till June 15

title: Transfer of Kerala's Director of Health Services kicks up a row
content: The government order transferring her had a statement that Dr. Reena had availed 15 days’ leave during the epidemic period, setting off the speculation that she was booted out for seeking leave at a time when the public health system was under considerable strain

title: Start-up demonstrates facade cleaning drone at NIT-T
content: The machine is a hexacopter-based drone equipped with six high-performance motors, each capable of generating up to 15 kg of thrust, can rise up to a height of 100 metres

title: Kerala’s zoonotic potential in the background of Nipah
content: The peak Nipah virus spillover risk in the State is from April to September, when the abundance of seasonal fruit-laden trees, increased bat foraging activity, bat breeding season and viral shedding dynamics coincide, increasing the risk of human exposure. This pattern has not changed in Kerala since the very first outbreak.

title: India, Nepal discuss cross-border rail connectivity
content: The standard operating procedures for the commencement of passenger train services on the Janakpur-Ayodhya section was one of the focus points during the engagement between the two sides

title: Library Science graduates face unemployment as schools rely on teachers
content: Despite norms mandating dedicated library staff, successive governments have delayed permanent recruitment for over two decades

title: 29 years after Uphaar tragedy, victims' association says no lessons learnt
content: The association held its annual havan and shanti paath in Green Park, opposite the former Uphaar cinema site, to remember the 59 people who died in the June 13, 1997 fire

title: Woman kills three-month-old daughter before ending own life in Chennai
content: Preliminary inquiries by the police revealed that Dharini had been undergoing treatment for depression since childhood and that her condition had deteriorated after the birth of her daughter

title: Delhi court declines interim relief to Indian Polo Association in Jaipur Polo Ground eviction case
content: "A similar kind of request was moved before the Learned Principal District and Sessions Judge, PHC and Delhi High Court, and no relief was granted to the appellant. Therefore, keeping in view judicial discipline and propriety, I am not inclined to stay the execution of the impugned order even till next date"

title: ASI survey uncovers 25 inscriptions, ancient rock art in Nallamala Tiger Reserve
content: The teams had in the last three days documented and copied 25 inscriptions dating from the 2nd century CE to the 16th century CE

title: Central team reaches Kozhikode, three contacts of Nipah infected person test negative for virus
content: Revenue Minister A.P. Anil Kumar, who is in-charge of Kozhikode, says there is no large-scale spread of the infection at present, no need to declare a containment zone, he says

title: Minister Narayana reviews bridge construction across Kondaveeti Vagu and Palavagu in Amaravati
content: We are constructing 69 bridges across Kondaveeti Vagu and Palavagu. Efforts are being made to ensure that rainwater flows without obstruction and that no area in Amaravati experiences water stagnation during the rainy season, Mr. Narayana said.

title: Parents should not allow children to use social media: Andhra Pradesh child rights panel
content: The panel submitted a note to the Minister for Education Nara Lokesh to study the possibilities of enactment of an exclusive law to protect the children from adult content and others

title: Stand-up comedians who cross limits need to be taught lesson: Sena MLC to bring motion in House
content: "A lesson should be taught to such stand-up comedians. They are crossing all limits and making fun of anyone. They say whatever they want. I will introduce a motion in the upcoming monsoon session against such acts," says Shiv Sena leader

title: Monsoon 2026 LIVE: IMD forecasts thunderstorm in 11 districts of Telangana
content: According to the IMD’s latest bulletin, thunderstorms accompanied with lightning and gusty winds are very likely to occur at isolated places in 11 districts of Telangana

title: ED conducts raids on TMC MLA Madan Mitra's properties to probe municipal recruitment 'scam'
content: It is alleged that Mr. Mitra received bribes in the form of cash and gold through middlemen in relation to the appointment of undeserving candidates for various posts to different municipalities, including civic bodies of Kamarhati and adjacent Titagarh, ED officials said

title: Vision 2031: Kerala to be made most women-friendly State; task force to investigate crime against children
content: Document moots new legislation on the lines of Disha Act in Andhra Pradesh to prevent crime against women

title: Assam Cabinet decides to stop Aadhaar issuance to those aged above 18: CM
content: The step is being taken to ensure that no illegal Bangladeshis get Aadhaar cards, says Assam Chief Minister

title: Union Home Minister Amit Shah directs committee on demographic change to study border districts
content: Chairing the meeting of the committee, Mr. Shah also asked it to visit border areas, metro cities and industrial towns for an assessment of changes because of illegal migration and other unnatural causes

title: Salem man arrested for sexually assaulting woman; police recover obscene clips from his phone
content: In a viral audio clip, a woman alleged that M. Manikandan (40) had sexually assaulted her, recorded it on his mobile phone and threatened to release the video online. Meanwhile, TVK said the accused is not associated with the party.

title: Hyosung TNS eyes ATM manufacturing plant in Tamil Nadu: Industries Minister
content: Minister S. Keerthana said in a post on X that she had a productive meeting with the leadership of Hyosung TNS in South Korea, discussing opportunities in Tamil Nadu and exploring potential collaborations in advanced manufacturing and technology sectors

title: Kerala’s fiscal status report: Its politics and economics
content: The report places considerable emphasis on Kerala’s outstanding liabilities. But the rising debt was largely a product of the Covid-19 pandemic, when the State made full use of the higher borrowing limit allowed by the Union

title: Thematic yoga for VB-G Ram G workers in Vizianagaram on July 14
content: Collector S. Ram Sundar Reddy said the programme is aimed to spread yoga among rural people and make them aware of its benefits

title: Andhra Pradesh Biodiversity Board plans Statewide rollout of OECMs
content: The OECMs planned include harnessing industrial greenbelts, development of micro-forests in cities such as Vijayawada, Tirupati, Visakhapatnam, Kurnool and Rajahmundry

title: When a small town sends one of its own to Raisina Hill
content: Educational institutions, healthcare facilities and infrastructure projects are proposed for Rairangpur and Mayurbhanj as President Droupadi Murmu’s hometown prepares for a new phase of growth

title: Trees worth lakhs illegally felled on Devakottai government hospital premises; tahsildar orders probe
content: On June 12, 2026, trees worth several lakhs of rupees located there were suddenly cut down without permission. Activist Suresh lodged a complaint with the district administration.

title: Palakkad division of Southern Railway collects ₹17 lakh in fine; suspends six licences for level crossing violations
content: The Palakkad division said LC gates are critical safety installations and the violations can lead to fatal consequences, disruption of train services, and substantial damage to railway property

title: NTA launches digital training programme for examination officials
content: Titled 'Pariksha Karmayogi: Capacity-Building Programme for Examination Officials', the programme has been designed to strengthen the capacity of examination functionaries and to ensure the smooth, secure and standardised conduct of offline, pen-and-paper based examinations, says NTA

title: From ‘Toxic’ to ‘666 Operation Dream Theatre’, films to watch out for from Kannada cinema
content: The Kannada film industry has had a quiet 2026. It needs a remarkable turnaround to end the year on a positive note.

title: Air Chief Marshal A.P. Singh joins Kiran flypast, surprises audience at Air Force Academy
content: Chief of Air Staff Air Chief Marshal Amar Preet Singh took part in three-aircraft Kiran formation during Combined Graduation Parade ceremony in Hyderabad

title: AIADMK plans to hold extensive, district-wise consultations soon
content: Such an exercise will be helpful for the party, which has to face not only byelections to five Assembly constituencies but also local bodies’ polls in eight months, says a researcher of the party

title: DRDO demonstrates advanced missile defence, anti-ship strike capabilities in three successful flight tests
content: It further mentioned that the multi-layered BMD capability was successfully demonstrated with interceptor missiles engaging and destroying their designated targets.

title: From zero to 26, estimation shows signs of Nilgiri Tahrs’ revival in Coimbatore forest division
content: The estimated increase in the population from zero to 26 from 2024 to 2026 indicates either improved population estimation efforts or recolonisation of suitable habitats, says the survey report

title: Farmers, environmentalists oppose proposal for hydrocarbon wells off Parangipettai coast
content: They warn the mangroves will be under threat if the project is implemented, and it will destroy coastal natural resources and severely affect the livelihood of fishermen

title: Padayatras or bus yatras won’t revive BRS, says Government whip Aadi Srinivas
content: The Congress leader alleged that during their decade in power, BRS leaders remained confined to Pragathi Bhavan and farmhouses, rarely interacting with the public

title: ‘CPI did not seek guidance or permission from anyone’
content: The resolution by the Left party is an apparent reference to DMK president M.K. Stalin’s recent remarks that the former alliance partners of the DMK had consulted him before extending support to TVK.

title: Umar Khalid, Sharjeel Imam move Delhi court for bail; police asked to respond
content: The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide. With an unwavering commitment to excellence in journalism, our team is at the forefront of gathering, verifying, and presenting breaking news, in-depth analysis, and insightful reports on a wide range of topics.
